debug_info.goal:
ι : Type u_1
Ω : Type u_2
E : Type u_3
mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω
P : Measure Ω
inst✝⁷ : LinearOrder ι
inst✝⁶ : OrderBot ι
𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ
X : ι → Ω → E
inst✝⁵ : TopologicalSpace ι
inst✝⁴ : OrderTopology ι
inst✝³ : NormedAddCommGroup E
inst✝² : 𝓕.HasUsualConditions P
inst✝¹ : NormedSpace ℝ E
inst✝ : CompleteSpace E
hX :
  @ProbabilityTheory.Locally ι Ω E mΩ inst✝⁷ inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴
    SubtractionMonoid.toSubNegZeroMonoid.toNegZeroClass.toZero
    (fun X => ∀ (ω : Ω) (x : ι), ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x => X x ω) (nhdsWithin x (Set.Iio x)) (nhds l)) 𝓕 X P
⊢ ∀ᵐ (ω : Ω) ∂P, ∀ (x : ι), ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x => X x ω) (nhdsWithin x (Set.Iio x)) (nhds l)

failed_attempts:
obtain ⟨τ, hτ, hX'⟩ := hX
  filter_upwards [hX' 0 (aet := by simpa using hX.aet)] with ω hω
  intro x
  have h₃ := hω x
  obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ := h₃
  exact ⟨l, hl⟩

have h₀ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
    have h₁ := hX.stoppedProcess
    filter_upwards [h₁] with ω hω x
    exact hω x
  exact h₀

have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
    have h₂ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) :=
      hX
      |>.with_local_seq (fun n ω ↦ ⊤)
      |>.mono fun X hX ↦ by
        simp_all [Filter.eventually_atTop]
        <;>
        (try aesop) <;>
        (try tauto) <;>
        (try simp_all [Filter.eventually_atTop]) <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try linarith)
      |>.rightContinuous
      |>.monotone
      |>.rightContinuous
      |>.leftContinuous
      |>.mono fun X hX ↦ by
        simp_all [Filter.eventually_atTop]
        <;>
        (try aesop) <;>
        (try tauto) <;>
        (try simp_all [Filter.eventually_atTop]) <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try linarith)
    exact h₂
  exact h₁
EXCEPTION: TimeoutError:

      have h₈ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by

have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
    have h₂ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
      -- Use the fact that the property holds locally
      filter_upwards [hX.ae] with ω
      intro x
      have h₃ : ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x => (X · ω) x) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
        have h₄ : ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x => (X · ω) x) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
          -- For each x, use the local property to find the limit
          intro x
          have h₅ : ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x => (X · ω) x) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
            have h₆ : ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x => (X · ω) x) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
              simpa using hX.2.property ω
            exact h₆ x
          exact h₅
        exact h₄
      have h₇ : ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x => (X · ω) x) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := h₃ x
      simpa using h₇
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

But this is not quite accurate. The definition actually depends on the exact definition used in the library. 

But in any case, if `τ n ω = ⊤`, then for every `y : ι`, `y ≤ ⊤` is true (since `ι` is a linear order with a bottom element), so `X^{τ n}(y, ω) = X(y, ω)` for all `y : ι`.

Thus, `X^{τ n}(·, ω)` and `X(·, ω)` are equal as functions `ι → E`, so their limits are the same.

have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
    have h₂ : ∃ (τ : ℕ → Ω → WithTop ι), IsLocalizingSequence 𝓕 τ P ∧ ∀ n, (fun X ↦ ∀ ω, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l)) (X.comp (fun i ↦ (τ n : ι → ι).iInf.pi)) := by
      exact hX.exists_localSeq
    rcases h₂ with ⟨τ, hτ, h₃⟩
    have h₄ : ∀ n, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · (X.comp (fun i ↦ (τ n : ι → ι).iInf.pi) · x)) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
      intro n x
      have h₅ : (fun X ↦ ∀ ω, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l)) (X.comp (fun i ↦ (τ n : ι → ι).iInf.pi)) := h₃ n
      have h₆ := h₅ (X · (X.comp (fun i ↦ (τ n : ι → ι).iInf.pi))) x
      exact h₆
    have h₅ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
      have h₆ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
        -- Use the localizing sequence to restrict to a subset where the property holds
        have h₇ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
          have h₈ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
            -- Use the fact that the localizing sequence is eventually stopping
            have h₉ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
              filter_upwards [hτ.tendsto_top] with ω hω
              intro x
              have h₁₀ := h₄ 0 x
              obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ := h₁₀
              refine' ⟨l, _⟩
              have h₁₁ : Tendsto (X · (X.comp (fun i ↦ (τ 0 : ι → ι).iInf.pi) · x)) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := hl
              have h₁₂ : X · (X.comp (fun i ↦ (τ 0 : ι → ι).iInf.pi) · x) = X · x := by
                ext i
                simp [Function.comp_apply]
                <;>
                (try simp_all [IsLocalizingSequence.tendsto_top, IsLocalizingSequence.isStoppingTime])
                <;>
                (try norm_num)
                <;>
                (try aesop)
              rw [h₁₂] at h₁₁
              exact h₁₁
            exact h₉
          exact h₈
        exact h₇
      exact h₆
    exact h₅
  exact h₁

have h₁ := hX.stoppedProcess
  simpa using h₁

have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
    have h₂ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (hX.localSeq 0 · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
      have h₃ : hX.of_localSeq 0 := hX.of_localSeq 0
      -- Use the fact that the property holds for the stopped process

        intro ω x
        -- Use the property of the localizing sequence
        have h₅ := h₃.2.1.1 x
        -- Simplify the tendsto condition
        simpa using h₅
      -- Use the fact that the property holds for the stopped process
      filter_upwards [hX.ae] with ω
      intro x
      exact h₄ ω x
    -- Use the fact that the property holds for the original process
    have h₃ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
      have h₄ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
        have h₅ : h₂ := h₂
        -- Use the fact that the property holds for the stopped process
        have h₆ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, hX.localSeq 0 · ω = X · ω := by
          filter_upwards [hX.ae] with ω
          have h₇ : hX.isLocalizingSequence.is_localization ω := hX.isLocalizingSequence.is_localization ω
          have h₈ : hX.localSeq 0 · ω = X · ω := by
            have h₉ := hX.isLocalizingSequence.tendsto_top ω
            simp_all [Filter.Tendsto, eventually_atTop]
            <;>
            (try simp_all [hX.isLocalizingSequence]) <;>
            (try aesop) <;>
            (try {
              simp_all [Filter.Tendsto, eventually_atTop]
              <;>
              aesop
            })
          simp_all
        filter_upwards [h₆] with ω hω
        intro x
        have h₇ : ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (hX.localSeq 0 · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
          intro x
          have h₈ := h₂
          simp_all [Filter.Tendsto, eventually_atTop]
          <;>
          aesop
        have h₈ : ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (hX.localSeq 0 · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := h₇
        have h₉ : ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
          intro x
          have h₁₀ : hX.localSeq 0 · ω = X · ω := hω
          have h₁₁ : ∃ l, Tendsto (hX.localSeq 0 · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := h₈ x
          obtain ⟨l, h₁₂⟩ := h₁₁
          have h₁₃ : Tendsto (hX.localSeq 0 · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := h₁₂
          have h₁₄ : Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
            have h₁₅ : hX.localSeq 0 · ω = X · ω := hω
            have h₁₆ : Tendsto (hX.localSeq 0 · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := h₁₃
            have h₁₇ : Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
              simpa [h₁₅] using h₁₆
            exact h₁₇
          exact ⟨l, h₁₄⟩
        exact h₉ x
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

    intro ω x
    have h₂ := hX.2.2
    have h₃ := h₂ 0
    have h₄ := h₃ ω
    have h₅ := h₄ x
    simpa using h₅

  intro x

have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
    obtain ⟨τ, hτ, h₂⟩ := hX
    have h₃ : ∀⁢ (n : ℕ), ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (fun i ↦ (X · ω) (min (τ n ω) i)) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
      intro n
      have h₄ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (fun i ↦ (X · ω) (min (τ n ω) i)) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
        filter_upwards [h₂ n] with ω hω
        intro x
        have h₅ : ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (fun i ↦ (X · ω) (min (τ n ω) i)) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
          simpa [Function.comp_apply] using hω
        exact h₅ x
      exact h₄
    filter_upwards [h₃ 0] with ω hω
    intro x
    have h₄ : ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (fun i ↦ (X · ω) (min (τ 0 ω) i)) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
      simpa [Function.comp_apply] using hω
    have h₅ : ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
      have h₆ := h₄ x
      simp only [Function.comp_apply] at h₆
      by_cases h : (τ 0 ω : WithTop ι) = ⊥
      · -- Case: τ 0 ω = ⊥

lemma Locally.left_limit
    (hX : Locally (fun X ↦ ∀ ω, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l)) 𝓕 X P) :
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
  -- We use the fact that the property holds locally to deduce it holds almost everywhere.
  have h₁ : ∀ᵐ (ω : Ω) ∂P, ∀ (x : ι), ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x => X x ω) (nhdsWithin x (Set.Iio x)) (nhds l) := by
    -- Extract the localizing sequence and the stopping time from the local property.
    obtain ⟨τ, hτ, hτ'⟩ := hX
    -- Use the stopping time to get the property almost everywhere.
    have h₂ : ∀ᵐ (ω : Ω) ∂P, ∀ (x : ι), ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x => X x ω) (nhdsWithin x (Set.Iio x)) (nhds l) := by
      filter_upwards [hX.ae] with ω hω
      -- For each ω, the property holds for the stopped process, which implies it holds for the original process.
      intro x
      have h₃ := hω x
      obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ := h₃
      exact ⟨l, hl⟩
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

**Formalization Strategy:**

1. Use `hX` to get `τ`, `hτ : IsLocalizingSequence 𝓕 τ P`, and `hstopped : ∀ n, ∀ ω, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (fun x ↦ (stoppedProcess X τ n) x ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l)`.

2. For `hτ.tendsto_top`, we have that for almost every `ω`, for any `x : ι`, there exists `n` such that `x ≤ τ n ω`. 

3. Therefore, for almost every `ω`, for any `x`, we can find `n` such that `x ≤ τ n ω`. 

4. For this `n`, and for any `y < x`, we have `y ≤ x ≤ τ n ω`, so `(stoppedProcess X τ n) y ω = X y ω`. 

5. Thus, the filter `𝓝[<] x` is mapped to itself by `fun y ↦ (stoppedProcess X τ n) y ω = X y ω`.

6. So if `Tendsto (fun y ↦ (stoppedProcess X τ n) y ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l)`, then `Tendsto (fun y ↦ X y ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l)`.

But for 5., we can directly use `Filter.EventuallyEq.tendsto`. 

**Final Proof Sketch:**

1. Obtain `τ`, `hτ`, `hstopped` from `hX`.
2. Since `hτ.tendsto_top`, for almost every `ω`, for any `x : ι`, there exists `n` such that `x ≤ τ n ω`. 
   - (Actually, stronger: for almost every `ω`, for any `x`, for sufficiently large `n`, `x ≤ τ n ω`.)
3. Fix such an `ω` and any `x`. Find `n` such that `x ≤ τ n ω`.
4. Use `hstopped n` to get `l` such that `Tendsto (fun y ↦ (stoppedProcess X τ n) y ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l)`.
5. Show that `(fun y ↦ (stoppedProcess X τ n) y ω)` and `(fun y ↦ X y ω)` are eventually equal on `𝓝[<] x`, because for `y < x`, `y ≤ τ n ω` (as `y < x ≤ τ n ω`).
6. Deduce that `Tendsto (fun y ↦ X y ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l)`.

**Implementation Note:**
In Lean, `IsLocalizingSequence.tendsto_top` is `Filter.Tendsto τ atTop (nhdsWithBot ⊤)` (i.e., for a.e. `ω`, `τ n ω → ∞` as `n → ∞`), so for any `x`, for sufficiently large `n`, `x ≤ τ n ω`. 

But perhaps `Filter.Tendsto` is not the right way to deduce it directly. 

Actually, perhaps a better approach is to use that for each `x`, there exists `n` such that for all `m ≥ n`, `x ≤ τ m ω`, because `τ m ω

have h : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ x, ∃ l, Tendsto (X · ω) (𝓝[<] x) (𝓝 l) := by
    filter_upwards [hX.tendsto_top] with ω hω
    intro x
    have h₁ := hω x
    simpa using h₁
  exact h

-- Show that the constant sequence |ι| is a valid localizing sequence
      have h₁ : ∀ i : ι, (|ι| : WithTop ι) ≥ i := by
        intro i
        simp [WithTop.coe_le_coe]
        <;> exact le_of_not_lt (by
          intro h₂
          -- Use the fact that the type ι is not empty to derive a contradiction
          have h₃ : Nonempty ι := ⟨i⟩
          simp_all [WithTop.coe_lt_coe]
          <;> omega)
      -- Use the fact that the sequence is constant to show it's a valid localizing sequence
      have h₂ : IsLocalizingSequence 𝓕 (fun _ ↦ (|ι| : WithTop ι)) P := by
        refine' { isStoppingTime := _, mono := _, tendsto_top := _ }
        · -- Prove that the stopping time is valid
          intro i
          have h₃ : MeasurableSet {ω : Ω | (|ι| : WithTop ι) ≤ i} := by
            simp [h₁]
            <;>
            (try simp_all [WithTop.measurableSet_top, WithTop.measurableSet_coe])
            <;>
            (try simp_all [measurableSet_univ])
          exact IsStoppingTime.of_ae (by
            simp_all [h₁]
            <;>
            (try simp_all [WithTop.measurableSet_top, WithTop.measurableSet_coe])
            <;>
            (try simp_all [measurableSet_univ]))
        · -- Prove that the sequence is monotonically increasing
          intro n m hnm
          simp_all [h₁]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [WithTop.measurableSet_top, WithTop.measurableSet_coe])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [measurableSet_univ])
        · -- Prove that the sequence tends to infinity
          simp_all [h₁]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [WithTop.measurableSet_top, WithTop.measurableSet_coe])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [measurableSet_univ])
      -- Use the fact that the sequence is constant to show it's a valid localizing sequence
      simp_all [h₂]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [WithTop.measurableSet_top, WithTop.measurableSet_coe])
      <;>
      (try simp_all [measurableSet_univ])⟩
    <;>
    (try simp_all [WithTop.measurableSet_top, WithTop.measurableSet_coe])
    <;>
    (try simp_all [measurableSet_univ])
  ⟩
